enforced.oracle: P(parse(x)) == P(parse(T(x))) where P extracts all meta-information lines as a set of key-value pairs, ignoring line order (except ##fileformat which stays first). The canonical JSON representation must be identical.

evidence.quote: Other than |##fileformat|, they may appear in any order.
Meta-information lines can be in any order, with the exception of ##fileformat which must come first.

enforced.mr_name: Structured meta-line key-value pair order invariance
enforced.oracle: P(parse(x)) == P(parse(T(x))) where P extracts the structured meta-line content as a set of key=value pairs, ignoring their order within the angle-bracketed definition.

evidence.quote: Implementations must not rely on the order of the fields within structured lines and are not required to preserve field ordering.
Implementations must not rely on the order of the fields within structured lines and are not required to preserve field ordering.

enforced.mr_name: Trim common affixes invariance — canonical parsimonious representation
enforced.oracle: P(parse(x)) == P(parse(T(x))) — the canonical normalized representation must be semantically identical; POS shifts by the number of trimmed prefix bases, REF and ALT are shortened accordingly, and the variant identity is preserved

evidence.quote: REF — reference base(s): Each base must be one of A,C,G,T,N (case insensitive). Multiple bases are permitted. The value in the POS field refers to the position of the first base in the String. For simple insertions and deletions in which either the REF or one of the ALT alleles would otherwise be null/empty, the REF and ALT Strings must include the base before the variant (which must be reflected in the POS field)

enforced.mr_name: Left-align indel in homopolymer run invariance
enforced.oracle: P(parse(x)) == P(parse(T(x))) — left-shifting an indel within a homopolymer run preserves the biological variant; POS decrements by 1, first base of REF/ALT is preserved, and the variant semantics are identical per Tan 2015 §2.1

evidence.quote: REF — reference base(s): Each base must be one of A,C,G,T,N (case insensitive). Multiple bases are permitted. The value in the POS field refers to the position of the first base in the String. For simple insertions and deletions in which either the REF or one of the ALT alleles would otherwise be null/empty, the REF and ALT Strings must include the base before the variant (which must be reflected in the POS field)

enforced.oracle: P(parse(x)) == P(parse(T(x))) — splitting a multi-ALT record into one record per ALT allele with remapped GT indices and synchronized Number=A/R INFO/FORMAT arrays preserves the same variant set per bcftools norm --multiallelics semantics

evidence.quote: ALT --- alternate base(s): Comma-separated list of alternate non-reference alleles. These alleles do not have to be called in any of the samples.

enforced.oracle: P(parse(x)) == P(parse(T(x))) — round-tripping through BCF2 binary encoding must preserve all variant records, genotypes, INFO/FORMAT values, and meta-information; any difference exposes a codec bug

evidence.quote: Note that BCF, the binary counterpart of VCF, requires that all entries are present.

enforced.mr_name: Permute BCF header dictionary order invariance
enforced.oracle: P(parse(x)) == P(parse(T(x))) — reordering ##contig/##INFO/##FORMAT/##FILTER dictionary entries in BCF encoding must not change the parsed representation; dictionary indices are implementation-defined and must be resolved by consulting the header

evidence.quote: Other than |##fileformat|, they may appear in any order. Note that BCF, the binary counterpart of VCF, requires that all entries are present.

enforced.mr_name: Reject fileformat not on first line
enforced.oracle: A compliant VCF parser MUST reject (raise a validation error, crash, or refuse to emit) a file where ##fileformat is not the first line. Silent acceptance or silent record-drop is a conformance bug.

evidence.quote: A single `fileformat' line is always required, must be the first line in the file, and details the VCF format version number.
Other than |##fileformat|, they may appear in any order.

enforced.oracle: A compliant VCF parser MUST reject (raise a validation error, crash, or refuse to emit) a record where an INFO field declared Number=A has a cardinality different from len(ALT). Silent acceptance or silent record-drop is a conformance bug.

evidence.quote: A: The field has one value per alternate allele. The values must be in the same order as listed in the ALT column
The Number entry is an Integer that describes the number of values that can be included with the INFO field.

enforced.oracle: After shuffling meta-information lines, all data records must remain in the same order, and POS values must remain sorted in increasing order within each CHROM reference sequence. The POS column values are unchanged by the transform.

evidence.quote: Note that the positions must be sorted in increasing order

enforced.oracle: parse(x) == parse(inject_equivalent_missing_values(x)); appending a FORMAT field filled with '.' to all samples is semantically a no-op

evidence.quote: If any of the fields is missing, it is replaced with the MISSING value.
If a field contains a list of missing values, it can be represented either as a single MISSING value (`.') or as a list of missing values (e.g. `.,.,.' if the field was Number=3).

enforced.mr_name: Meta-line shuffle preserves variant query results
enforced.oracle: P(parse(x)) == P(parse(T(x))) for each listed query method — meta-information lines (other than ##fileformat) may appear in any order per spec, so shuffling them must not change any parsed record's query results.

evidence.quote: Other than |##fileformat|, they may appear in any order.
Other than |##fileformat|, they may appear in any order.

enforced.mr_name: Structured meta-line field permutation preserves variant query results
enforced.oracle: P(parse(x)) == P(parse(T(x))) for each listed query method — implementations must not rely on the order of fields within structured meta-information lines.

evidence.quote: Implementations must not rely on the order of the fields within structured lines and are not required to preserve field ordering.
Implementations must not rely on the order of the fields within structured lines and are not required to preserve field ordering.

enforced.mr_name: INFO field key-value shuffle preserves variant query results
enforced.oracle: P(parse(x)) == P(parse(T(x))) for each listed query method — the order of semicolon-separated key=value pairs in the INFO column is not semantically meaningful per VCF spec.

evidence.quote: Implementations must not rely on the order of the fields within structured lines and are not required to preserve field ordering.

enforced.mr_name: ALT allele permutation with GT remapping preserves variant query results
enforced.oracle: P(parse(x)) == P(parse(T(x))) for each listed query method — permuting ALT alleles with corresponding GT index remapping and Number=A/R field reordering preserves the same variant semantics.

evidence.quote: A: The field has one value per alternate allele. The values must be in the same order as listed in the ALT column (described in section <ref>).
R: The field has one value for each possible allele, including the reference. The order of the values must be the reference allele first, then the alternate alleles as listed in the ALT column.
A: The field has one value per alternate allele. The values must be in the same order as listed in the ALT column (described in section data-lines).

quarantine.mr_name: INFO field key-value pair order invariance
quarantine.oracle: P(parse(x)) == P(parse(T(x))) where P extracts INFO field content as a set of key=value pairs, ignoring the semicolon-separated ordering.

evidence.quote: Implementations must not rely on the order of the fields within structured lines and are not required to preserve field ordering.

ambiguity_flags: The spec does not explicitly state that INFO key=value pairs in data lines have no ordering semantics, but by analogy with structured header lines and common practice, order is not semantically meaningful.

quarantine.oracle: P(parse(x)) == P(parse(T(x))) where P extracts sample genotype data as a set keyed by sample ID, ignoring column ordering.

evidence.quote: Other than |##fileformat|, they may appear in any order.

ambiguity_flags: The VCF spec does not explicitly state that sample column order is semantically irrelevant, but sample identity is determined by the column header ID, not position.

quarantine.mr_name: ALT allele permutation with synchronized GT and Number=A/R fields
quarantine.oracle: P(parse(x)) == P(parse(T(x))) where P extracts the variant representation with ALT alleles as a set and GT indices remapped accordingly. The biological variant semantics are preserved.

evidence.quote: A: The field has one value per alternate allele. The values must be in the same order as listed in the ALT column (described in section <ref>).

ambiguity_flags: The spec mandates Number=A values follow ALT order, so permuting ALT requires synchronized remapping of Number=A and Number=R values and GT indices.

quarantine.oracle: A compliant VCF parser MUST reject (raise a validation error, crash, or refuse to emit) a data line that does not have all 8 mandatory fixed columns (CHROM, POS, ID, REF, ALT, QUAL, FILTER, INFO). Silent acceptance or silent record-drop is a conformance bug.

evidence.quote: The mandatory header line names the 8 fixed, mandatory columns. These columns are as follows: #CHROM POS ID REF ALT QUAL FILTER INFO
Zero length fields are not allowed, a dot (``.'') must be used instead.

quarantine.mr_name: Reject GT allele index out of bounds
quarantine.oracle: A compliant VCF parser MUST reject (raise a validation error, crash, or refuse to emit) a record where a GT allele index references an ALT allele that does not exist (index > len(ALT)). Silent acceptance or silent record-drop is a conformance bug.

evidence.quote: For one individual, each integer in the vector is organized as (allele+1) << 1 | phased where allele is set to -1 if the allele in GT is a dot `.'
ALT --- alternate base(s): Comma-separated list of alternate non-reference alleles.

quarantine.oracle: After parse-write-parse round-trip through the primary SUT's writer, the POS column values must be identical to the original. The 1-based coordinate system must be preserved. END INFO field (if present) must also be preserved. All data records must remain sorted in increasing POS order within each CHROM.

evidence.quote: * A single base insertion of A after position 3 becomes REF=C, ALT=CA
Note that the positions must be sorted in increasing order

This is a computed field that, when present, must be set to the maximum end reference position (1-based) of: the position of the final base of the REF allele, the end position corresponding to the SVLEN of a symbolic SV allele, and the end positions calculated from FORMAT LEN for the <*> symbolic allele.
